heading: Loan guarantees for Native Hawaiian housing
label: 1715z–13b Loan guarantees for Native Hawaiian housing

children.heading: Department of Hawaiian Home Lands
children.label: 1 Department of Hawaiian Home Lands
children.text: The term “Department of Hawaiian Home Lands” means the agency or department of the government of the State of Hawaii that is responsible for the administration of the Hawaiian Homes Commission Act, 1920 ( et seq.).42 Stat. 108

children.heading: Eligible entity
children.label: 2 Eligible entity
children.text: The term “eligible entity” means a Native Hawaiian family, the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands, the Office of Hawaiian Affairs, and private nonprofit or private for-profit organizations experienced in the planning and development of affordable housing for Native Hawaiians.

children.heading: Family
children.label: 3 Family
children.text: The term “family” means one or more persons maintaining a household, as the Secretary shall by regulation provide.

children.heading: Guarantee Fund
children.label: 4 Guarantee Fund
children.text: The term “Guarantee Fund” means the Native Hawaiian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund established under subsection (j).

children.label: A
children.text: have the status of Hawaiian Home Lands under section 204 of the Hawaiian Homes Commission Act (); or42 Stat. 110

children.label: B
children.text: are acquired pursuant to that Act.

children.label: A
children.text: a citizen of the United States; and

children.label: i
children.text: genealogical records;

children.label: ii
children.text: verification by kupuna (elders) or kama’aina (long-term community residents); or

children.label: iii
children.text: birth records of the State of Hawaii.

children.heading: Office of Hawaiian Affairs
children.label: 7 Office of Hawaiian Affairs
children.text: The term “Office of Hawaiian Affairs” means the entity of that name established under the constitution of the State of Hawaii.

children.heading: Authority
children.label: b Authority
children.text: To provide access to sources of private financing to Native Hawaiian families who otherwise could not acquire housing financing because of the unique legal status of the Hawaiian Home Lands or as a result of a lack of access to private financial markets, the Secretary may guarantee an amount not to exceed 100 percent of the unpaid principal and interest that is due on an eligible loan under subsection (c).

children.label: A
children.text: a Native Hawaiian family;

children.label: B
children.text: the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands;

children.label: C
children.text: the Office of Hawaiian Affairs; or

children.label: D
children.text: a private nonprofit organization experienced in the planning and development of affordable housing for Native Hawaiians.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The loan will be used to construct, acquire, or rehabilitate not more than 4-family dwellings that are standard housing and are located on Hawaiian Home Lands for which a housing plan described in subparagraph (B) applies.

children.label: i
children.text: has been submitted and approved by the Secretary under ; andsection 4223 of title 25

children.label: ii
children.text: provides for the use of loan guarantees under this section to provide affordable homeownership housing on Hawaiian Home Lands.

children.heading: Security
children.label: 3 Security
children.text: The loan may be secured by any collateral authorized under applicable Federal or State law.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The loan shall be made only by a lender approved by, and meeting qualifications established by, the Secretary, including any lender described in subparagraph (B), except that a loan otherwise insured or guaranteed by an agency of the Federal Government or made by the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands from amounts borrowed from the United States shall not be eligible for a guarantee under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: Any mortgagee approved by the Secretary for participation in the single family mortgage insurance program under title II of the National Housing Act [ et seq.].12 U.S.C. 1707

children.label: ii
children.text: Any lender that makes housing loans under chapter 37 of title 38 that are automatically guaranteed under .section 3702(d) of title 38

children.label: iii
children.text: Any lender approved by the Secretary of Agriculture to make guaranteed loans for single family housing under the Housing Act of 1949 [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1441

children.label: iv
children.text: Any other lender that is supervised, approved, regulated, or insured by any agency of the Federal Government.

children.label: A
children.text: be made for a term not exceeding 30 years;

children.label: B
children.text: bear interest (exclusive of the guarantee fee under subsection (e) and service charges, if any) at a rate agreed upon by the borrower and the lender and determined by the Secretary to be reasonable, but not to exceed the rate generally charged in the area (as determined by the Secretary) for home mortgage loans not guaranteed or insured by any agency or instrumentality of the Federal Government;

children.label: i
children.text: 97.75 percent of the appraised value of the property as of the date the loan is accepted for guarantee (or 98.75 percent if the value of the property is $50,000 or less); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount approved by the Secretary under this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: in cash or its equivalent; or

children.label: ii
children.text: through the value of any improvements to the property made through the skilled or unskilled labor of the borrower, as the Secretary shall provide.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Before the Secretary approves any loan for guarantee under this section, the lender shall submit the application for the loan to the Secretary for examination.

children.heading: Approval
children.label: B Approval
children.text: If the Secretary approves the application submitted under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall issue a certificate under this subsection as evidence of the loan guarantee approved.

children.heading: Standard for approval
children.label: 2 Standard for approval
children.text: The Secretary may approve a loan for guarantee under this section and issue a certificate under this subsection only if the Secretary determines that there is a reasonable prospect of repayment of the loan.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A certificate of guarantee issued under this subsection by the Secretary shall be conclusive evidence of the eligibility of the loan for guarantee under this section and the amount of that guarantee.

children.heading: Evidence
children.label: B Evidence
children.text: The evidence referred to in subparagraph (A) shall be incontestable in the hands of the bearer.

children.heading: Full faith and credit
children.label: C Full faith and credit
children.text: The full faith and credit of the United States is pledged to the payment of all amounts agreed to be paid by the Secretary as security for the obligations made by the Secretary under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: to preclude the Secretary from establishing defenses against the original lender based on fraud or material misrepresentation; or

children.label: B
children.text: to bar the Secretary from establishing by regulations that are on the date of issuance or disbursement, whichever is earlier, partial defenses to the amount payable on the guarantee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall fix and collect a guarantee fee for the guarantee of a loan under this section, which may not exceed the amount equal to 1 percent of the principal obligation of the loan.

children.label: A
children.text: be paid by the lender at time of issuance of the guarantee; and

children.label: B
children.text: be adequate, in the determination of the Secretary, to cover expenses and probable losses.

children.heading: Deposit
children.label: 3 Deposit
children.text: The Secretary shall deposit any fees collected under this subsection in the Native Hawaiian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund established under subsection (j).

children.heading: Liability under guarantee
children.label: f Liability under guarantee
children.text: The liability under a guarantee provided under this section shall decrease or increase on a pro rata basis according to any decrease or increase in the amount of the unpaid obligation under the provisions of the loan agreement involved.

children.heading: Transfer and assumption
children.label: g Transfer and assumption
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any loan guaranteed under this section, including the security given for the loan, may be sold or assigned by the lender to any financial institution subject to examination and supervision by an agency of the Federal Government or of any State or the District of Columbia.

children.heading: Disqualification of lenders and civil money penalties
children.label: h Disqualification of lenders and civil money penalties
I
children.text: to maintain adequate accounting records;

children.label: II
children.text: to service adequately loans guaranteed under this section; or

children.label: III
children.text: to exercise proper credit or underwriting judgment; or

children.label: ii
children.text: has engaged in practices otherwise detrimental to the interest of a borrower or the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: refuse, either temporarily or permanently, to guarantee any further loans made by such lender or holder;

children.label: ii
children.text: bar such lender or holder from acquiring additional loans guaranteed under this section; and

children.label: iii
children.text: require that such lender or holder assume not less than 10 percent of any loss on further loans made or held by the lender or holder that are guaranteed under this section.

children.heading: Civil money penalties for intentional violations
children.label: 2 Civil money penalties for intentional violations
i
children.text: to maintain adequate accounting records;

children.label: ii
children.text: to adequately service loans guaranteed under this section; or

children.label: iii
children.text: to exercise proper credit or underwriting judgment.

children.heading: Penalties
children.label: B Penalties
children.text: A civil monetary penalty imposed under this paragraph shall be imposed in the manner and be in an amount provided under section 536 of the National Housing Act [] with respect to mortgagees and lenders under that Act.12 U.S.C. 1735f–14

children.heading: Payment on loans made in good faith
children.label: 3 Payment on loans made in good faith
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraphs (1) and (2), if a loan was made in good faith, the Secretary may not refuse to pay a lender or holder of a valid guarantee on that loan, without regard to whether the lender or holder is barred under this subsection.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: i Notification
children.text: If a borrower on a loan guaranteed under this section defaults on the loan, the holder of the guarantee certificate shall provide written notice of the default to the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: aa In general
children.text: The holder of the certificate may initiate foreclosure proceedings (after providing written notice of that action to the Secretary).

children.heading: Payment
children.label: bb Payment
children.text: Upon a final order by the court authorizing foreclosure and submission to the Secretary of a claim for payment under the guarantee, the Secretary shall pay to the holder of the certificate the pro rata portion of the amount guaranteed (as determined pursuant to subsection (f)) plus reasonable fees and expenses as approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: Subrogation
children.label: cc Subrogation
children.text: The rights of the Secretary shall be subrogated to the rights of the holder of the guarantee. The holder shall assign the obligation and security to the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: aa In general
children.text: Without seeking foreclosure (or in any case in which a foreclosure proceeding initiated under clause (i) continues for a period in excess of 1 year), the holder of the guarantee may submit to the Secretary a request to assign the obligation and security interest to the Secretary in return for payment of the claim under the guarantee. The Secretary may accept assignment of the loan if the Secretary determines that the assignment is in the best interest of the United States.

children.heading: Payment
children.label: bb Payment
children.text: Upon assignment, the Secretary shall pay to the holder of the guarantee the pro rata portion of the amount guaranteed (as determined under subsection (f)).

children.heading: Subrogation
children.label: cc Subrogation
children.text: The rights of the Secretary shall be subrogated to the rights of the holder of the guarantee. The holder shall assign the obligation and security to the Secretary.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: B Requirements
children.text: Before any payment under a guarantee is made under subparagraph (A), the holder of the guarantee shall exhaust all reasonable possibilities of collection. Upon payment, in whole or in part, to the holder, the note or judgment evidencing the debt shall be assigned to the United States and the holder shall have no further claim against the borrower or the United States. The Secretary shall then take such action to collect as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If a borrower defaults on a loan guaranteed under this section that involves a security interest in restricted Hawaiian Home Land property, the mortgagee or the Secretary shall only pursue liquidation after offering to transfer the account to another eligible Hawaiian family or the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: If, after action is taken under subparagraph (A), the mortgagee or the Secretary subsequently proceeds to liquidate the account, the mortgagee or the Secretary shall not sell, transfer, or otherwise dispose of or alienate the property described in subparagraph (A) except to another eligible Hawaiian family or to the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands.

children.heading: Hawaiian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund
children.label: j Hawaiian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States the Hawaiian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund for the purpose of providing loan guarantees under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: any amount, claims, notes, mortgages, contracts, and property acquired by the Secretary under this section, and any collections and proceeds therefrom;

children.label: B
children.text: any amounts appropriated pursuant to paragraph (7);

children.label: C
children.text: any guarantee fees collected under subsection (e); and

children.label: D
children.text: any interest or earnings on amounts invested under paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: fulfilling any obligations of the Secretary with respect to loans guaranteed under this section, including the costs (as that term is defined in ) of such loans;section 661a of title 2

children.label: B
children.text: paying taxes, insurance, prior liens, expenses necessary to make fiscal adjustment in connection with the application and transmittal of collections, and other expenses and advances to protect the Secretary for loans which are guaranteed under this section or held by the Secretary;

children.label: C
children.text: acquiring such security property at foreclosure sales or otherwise;

children.label: D
children.text: paying administrative expenses in connection with this section; and

children.label: E
children.text: reasonable and necessary costs of rehabilitation and repair to properties that the Secretary holds or owns pursuant to this section.

children.heading: Investment
children.label: 4 Investment
children.text: Any amounts in the Guarantee Fund determined by the Secretary to be in excess of amounts currently required at the time of the determination to carry out this section may be invested in obligations of the United States.

children.heading: Limitation on commitments to guarantee loans and mortgages
children.label: 5 Limitation on commitments to guarantee loans and mortgages

children.heading: Requirement of appropriations
children.label: A Requirement of appropriations
children.text: The authority of the Secretary to enter into commitments to guarantee loans under this section shall be effective for any fiscal year to the extent, or in such amounts as are, or have been, provided in appropriations Acts, without regard to the fiscal year for which such amounts were appropriated.

children.heading: Limitations on costs of guarantees
children.label: B Limitations on costs of guarantees
children.text: The authority of the Secretary to enter into commitments to guarantee loans under this section shall be effective for any fiscal year only to the extent that amounts in the Guarantee Fund are or have been made available in appropriations Acts to cover the costs (as that term is defined in ) of such loan guarantees for such fiscal year. Any amounts appropriated pursuant to this subparagraph shall remain available until expended.section 661a of title 2

children.heading: Limitation on outstanding aggregate principal amount
children.label: C Limitation on outstanding aggregate principal amount
children.text: Subject to the limitations in subparagraphs (A) and (B), the Secretary may enter into commitments to guarantee loans under this section for each of fiscal years 2001, 2002, 2003, 2004, and 2005 with an aggregate outstanding principal amount not exceeding $100,000,000 for each such fiscal year.

children.heading: Liabilities
children.label: 6 Liabilities
children.text: All liabilities and obligations of the assets credited to the Guarantee Fund under paragraph (2)(A) shall be liabilities and obligations of the Guarantee Fund.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 7 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Guarantee Fund to carry out this section such sums as may be necessary for each of fiscal years 2001, 2002, 2003, 2004, and 2005.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall, by regulation, establish housing safety and quality standards to be applied for use under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: provide sufficient flexibility to permit the use of various designs and materials in housing acquired with loans guaranteed under this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: be decent, safe, sanitary, and modest in size and design;

children.label: ii
children.text: conform with applicable general construction standards for the region in which the housing is located;

children.label: I
children.text: uses a properly installed system of piping;

children.label: II
children.text: includes a kitchen sink and a partitional bathroom with lavatory, toilet, and bath or shower; and

children.label: III
children.text: uses water supply, plumbing, and sewage disposal systems that conform to any minimum standards established by the applicable county or State;

children.label: iv
children.text: contain an electrical system using wiring and equipment properly installed to safely supply electrical energy for adequate lighting and for operation of appliances that conforms to any appropriate county, State, or national code;

children.label: v
children.text: be not less than the size provided under the applicable locally adopted standards for size of dwelling units, except that the Secretary, upon request of the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands may waive the size requirements under this paragraph; and

children.label: vi
children.text: conform with the energy performance requirements for new construction established by the Secretary under section 526(a) of the National Housing Act [], unless the Secretary determines that the requirements are not applicable.12 U.S.C. 1735f–4(a)

children.heading: Applicability of civil rights statutes
children.label: l Applicability of civil rights statutes
children.text: To the extent that the requirements of title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ( et seq.) or of the Fair Housing Act [ et seq.] apply to a guarantee provided under this subsection, nothing in the requirements concerning discrimination on the basis of race shall be construed to prevent the provision of the guarantee to an eligible entity on the basis that the entity serves Native Hawaiian families or is a Native Hawaiian family.42 U.S.C. 2000d42 U.S.C. 3601
